human_verification.notes: Verified cyber recovery specialty boundary, authoritative source coverage, required tenant facts, systems of record, output fidelity, refusal rules, low-confidence escalation, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

source_classes_covered: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

review_notes: All 18 scenarios pass with explicit cyber recovery evidence, advisory recommendation, output fidelity, refusal, escalation, conflicting-source, and tenant-specific behavior.
All required abilities across shared and cross-industry layers for the general workflow phase are fully covered.

specialist_owned_evidence: Cyber recovery outputs are tied to named BIA, criticality, RTO/RPO, dependency, backup, restore, incident-impact, owner, policy, and control records.

The lane refuses live incident response, privileged restoration, penetration testing, malware analysis, forensic acquisition, formal attestation, legal/regulatory conclusions, broad architecture ownership, and tenant-specific runbook changes.
Recovery release, failover, backup deletion, retention override, clean-system claims, and readiness certification require authorized owner evidence outside this lane.
Output shapes include prioritized recovery plan, restoration roadmap, escalation and rollback guidance, acceptance criteria checklist, executive memo, and out-of-scope handoff recommendation.
Runtime remains specification-based; retained generated implementation requires separate frequent-use promotion evidence.
notes: Structured evidence supports a validated spec-pack claim without claiming retained implementation ownership.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains after constraining sector, safety, legal, audit, release, and architecture questions to explicit routing.
